assistant_message.content:
{
 "finding_id": "openscap_055",
  "rule_id": "Restrict Exposed Kernel Pointer Addresses Access",
  "category": "safe_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.92,
  "rationale": "The finding modifies a sysctl parameter to restrict kernel pointer exposure; the change can be applied persistently via /etc/sysctl.d without reboot and does not affect authentication, networking, or filesystems. It follows standard STIG hardening patterns for kernel hardening and poses minimal risk of lockout.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "Potential reduction in kernel debugging information",
    "None that would cause service outage or lockout"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Create or edit a file in /etc/sysctl.d to set kernel.kptr_restrict= value",
    "Apply with 'sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/<filename>'",
    "Verify with 'sysctl kernel.kptr_restrict'"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": false,
  "touches_authn_authz": false,
  "touches_networking": false,
  "touches_filesystems": false
}
